records.ety: [Cf. F. vers, L. versatus, p. p. of versari to turn about frequently, to turn over, to be engaged in a thing, passive of versare. See Versant, a.]

defs: Acquainted or familiar, as the result of experience, study, practice, etc.; skilled; practiced; knowledgeable; expert.
quotes: Deep versed in books and shallow in himself.
Opinions . . . derived from studying the Scriptures, wherein he was versed beyond any person of his age.
These men were versed in the details of business.

records.ety: [L. versus turned, p. p. vertere. See 1st Versed.]
